item.title: AMLBot Puts Polymarket Phishing Toll at $3.1M Across 11 Wallets, Funds Traced to Ethereum
item.description: Blockchain intelligence firm AMLBot has confirmed the Polymarket supply-chain attack total at approximately $3.1 million in PUSD across 11 user wallets, with funds bridged from Polygon to Ethereum and converted to ETH. Polymarket has pledged full refunds but has not named the compromised vendor.

item.title: Polymarket Confirms $3 Million Loss From Third-Party Front-End Supply-Chain Breach
item.description: Polymarket confirmed hackers drained approximately $3 million through a compromised third-party vendor that injected malicious code into the platform's website, and pledged full refunds to fewer than 15 affected accounts.

item.title: Each privacy protocol can be evaluated from a CROPS perspective and when it comes to the S (Security), one thing that matters is trusted setup robustness. Our new Privacy page surfaces it for the pool
item.description: pEach privacy protocol can be evaluated from a CROPS perspective and when it comes to the S (Security), one thing that matters is trusted setup robustness. Our new Privacy page surfaces it for the pools we support.br /br /A short thread on what they are and why the numbers matter. 👇 🧵/pimg src="http

item.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
item.description: After a global lull, ransomware gangs are setting sights on a rich new arena: attacking EU organizations and their suppliers.
